title: SQL injection vulnerability detected in product search endpoint
description: A penetration test has revealed a SQL injection vulnerability in the GET /api/products/search endpoint. The 'q' query parameter is being interpolated directly into a raw SQL query without parameterization. An attacker can extract sensitive data or modify the database.
steps_to_reproduce: Observe that all products are returned instead of search results
Send GET /api/products/search?q=test' UNION SELECT id,email,password_hash,null,null FROM users--
Observe that user credentials are leaked in the response
error_log: No errors — the injection executes successfully, which is the problem.
expected_behavior: Search query is parameterized and only returns matching products
actual_behavior: Raw SQL string interpolation allows arbitrary SQL execution
recent_changes: Search endpoint was added as a quick feature with raw SQL for 'performance'
